summary: The test suite covers all major requirements: SHA-256 hashing, tree scanning, tree comparison, sync plan generation (all action types), dry-run mode, execute mode, and a full end-to-end integration test with RealFileSystem. Edge cases are well-represented — missing files raise errors, nested directory creation is verified, identical files produce no actions, and a mixed-diff scenario exercises all four categories simultaneously. The helper `_make_diff` and `_make_plan` factory methods keep tests concise and readable, and each test class maps cleanly to a single module component. The only minor gap is the absence of error-path tests for execute_sync itself (e.g., unknown action type, source file missing during COPY) and no test for scanning a directory that contains only subdirectories with no files, but these are minor omissions in an otherwise rigorous suite. Independence is good — each test builds its own MockFileSystem or temp directory with no shared mutable state. The integration test with tearDown cleanup is correctly structured. Overall a high-quality test suite that would reliably catch regressions.
